prompt:
Billing keeps asking why customers on the Starter plan ran 40k API calls in a month that caps at 10k, and the honest answer is our "quota system" is an if-statement in the gateway. I want a real entitlement library the gateway and the workers can both link. Module go-quotasvc, package quota, stdlib only. My guess is our old counter got reset by a deploy, but either way this replaces it.

Shape I want:
- NewService(now func() time.Time) *Service — nil means time.Now. Every period decision goes through that clock; CI simulates months in microseconds, so no calls to time.Now anywhere else.
- Usage periods are UTC calendar months. When the clock enters a new month (mind December→January), an account's counters start from zero — no cron, no explicit reset call, it just happens on the next touch.
- DefinePlan(Plan) error with Plan{Name string; Limits map[string]int64; Overage map[string]OveragePolicy}. A metric absent from Limits is unlimited. Overage picks what happens when a request would push past the limit: OverageBlock (refuse the whole request, meter untouched — no partial consumption) or OverageAllowFlagged (let it through, keep counting past the limit, flag it so billing can invoice overage). Metrics not in the Overage map default to OverageBlock. Reject empty plan names, negative limits, and redefining a name that exists.
- SetAccount(accountID, planName string) error — assigns/changes the plan. A mid-month plan change keeps the month's usage; only the limits change.
- Record(accountID, metric string, n int64) (Decision, error) with Decision{Allowed bool; OverLimit bool; Remaining int64}. n must be >= 1. Remaining is what's left after the call (never negative; -1 for unlimited metrics). Unknown account, empty metric, bad n = errors, not decisions.
- Usage(accountID) (map[string]int64, error) — this month's counters, as a copy I can't corrupt.
- Report(accountID) ([]MetricReport, error) with MetricReport{Metric string; Used, Limit, Remaining int64; OverLimit bool}, sorted by metric name: one row per metric in the plan's Limits (even untouched ones) plus any unlimited metrics that have usage (Limit and Remaining -1 there).

The gateway calls Record from a lot of goroutines at once and a blocked limit must be exact — 100 concurrent singles against a limit of 50 lets exactly 50 through, never 51. CI is go test -race -timeout 30s ./... and quota_test.go + rollover_test.go are the acceptance contract, do not modify them. Structurally: plans/validation, the metering+decision path, period handling, and reporting should not all live in one file.
